subject: Human Resources
title: The Return-to-Office Debate
description: A quick, practical guide to The Return-to-Office Debate.

hook.text: A CEO mandates return to office five days a week. Thirty%of workers start job searching within a week.

buildup.text: Many companies pushed return-to-office mandates in 2023-2024—citing collaboration, culture, and real estate investments.

discovery.text: Research shows forced RTO correlates with higher attrition—especially among senior staff, women, and top performers.

twist.text: Some RTO mandates are quiet layoffs in disguise—making conditions uncomfortable enough that people quit without severance costs.

climax.text: The future isn't fully remote or fully office—it's about matching work arrangements to what actually drives results for each role.

punchline.text: Mandating butts in seats doesn't mandate productivity. Outcomes matter more than optics.

quiz.question: Who is most likely to leave after forced return-to-office mandates?
options: Senior employees, women, and top performers with options
